title: Pushword Repurpose Carousel
description: A social-media carousel derived from a Pushword page, stored as one JSON file per (page, network) and rendered to self-contained SVG slides. The Symfony Validator on the Carousel model is the source of truth; this schema mirrors it as an authoring aid. Cross-field rules it enforces but this schema only hints at: `format` must be one the `network` accepts (see /api/repurpose/networks); slide count and caption length must stay within the network's hard limits; each slide must show at least one text field or a background image; a text overlay only applies over an image. Colours and fonts left unset inherit the site's own design tokens.

page.description: The source page, addressed by its slug (host is the containing folder).

network.description: Target platform; selects the publishing rules (allowed formats, limits, export kind).

format.description: Output size; must be allowed for the chosen network.

plannedAt.description: Parseable date-time when the post is scheduled, or null.

caption.description: The post caption; length capped per network.

palette.description: Deck-level colour override; any slide may override again.

fontPairing.description: A key from the font-pairing registry (see /api/repurpose/networks, fontPairings). Only pick a pairing whose `installed` is true — an uninstalled one silently falls back to Roboto; `pw:repurpose:fonts <pairing>` installs the missing TTFs. Null inherits site typography.

creator.description: A creator key from repurpose_creators, or an inline {name,…} object. Null defaults from the page author, then the brand.

layout.description: Vertical anchor of the text block.

overlay.description: Darken the image behind the text (only valid with an image). Defaults to 0.35 on a slide with an image (0 without) so light text stays readable; set 0 explicitly for no overlay — validate flags low text/background contrast as a warning.

media.description: Media filename from the site library.

focusX.description: Horizontal focal point kept in frame.

focusY.description: Vertical focal point kept in frame.
